title: James Street TIP, Chaffi Bridge, Hazard Mitigation, Parks Capital, Inner Harbor Railings $3M, syr.gov Hosting
summary: Omnibus segment: (1) Item 21 -- CNS Engineers $29K increase for James Street improvement TIP (South Salina to Grant Blvd), construction ~2028. (2) Chaffi Ave pedestrian bridge reapplication (failed 2024, connected to Creek Walk Phase 3). (3) Item 23 -- Onondaga County Hazard Mitigation Plan adoption, required for FEMA 15% flood insurance discount; debate about neighborhoods added to flood zones that haven't flooded. (4) Item 24 -- Rose Society $2K plaque donation. (5) Items 25/56/57 -- Inner Harbor railings $3M comprehensive replacement, code-noncompliant, needed to permit festivals when Clinton Square loses access to I-81 in 2027; 27,000 attended Pride there last year. (6) Items 54/55 -- Progress Park playground $500K, first all-inclusive ADA playground with shipping container rental stand. (7) Item 26 -- 200 hemlock trees. (8) Item 27 -- Connect Kids $20K field trip grant. (9) syr.gov website hosting co-terming to fiscal year. Commissioner Saiisha Burke and Josh Wilcox present parks items.

political_signal.description: $3M Inner Harbor railings and $500K inclusive playground advancing with I-81 displacement urgency -- Clinton Square unavailable for festivals 2027

processing_notes: Very large omnibus segment covering 9+ agenda items. Inner Harbor railing replacement urgent because Clinton Square unavailable for festivals in 2027 due to I-81 construction. 27,000 attended Pride at Inner Harbor last year. Teal/James Street intersection identified as Vision Zero intersection. James St TIP design ~2028 start. Chaffi bridge reapplication after 2024 denial. Amphitheater deck needs full replacement but not part of this project. Parks public art item ($35K) missing from agenda -- to be added as waiver.
Segment 6 of 23 from Common Council Study Session, Wednesday March 18th 2026
Transcript corrections applied: CNS -> CNS Engineers; SU -> Syracuse University
Potential duplicate detected: overlaps with CTX-COUNCIL-2026-00060 -- linked, not skipped
